back_translation: 'But now they mock me, men younger than I, whose fathers I would have refused to put with my sheep dogs.
What use to me was the strength of their hands, since their vigor was gone?
Gaunt from want and hunger, they gnawed the dry land, in the gloom of desolate wasteland by night.
They plucked saltwort among the bushes, and the roots of the broom tree were their food.
They were driven out from among men and shouted at like thieves.
So they lived on the slopes of the ravines, in holes in the ground and among the rocks.
They brayed among the bushes and huddled together beneath the nettles.
A senseless and nameless brood, they were whipped and driven out of the land.
And now I have become their mocking song; I have become a byword among them.
They abhor me and keep their distance from me, and do not hesitate to spit in my face.
Because God has loosened the bowstring of my life and afflicted me, they have cast off all restraint before me.
The rabble rises up at my right; they set traps for my feet and build siege ramps to attack me.
They tear up my path and promote my ruin, with no one to restrain them.
They advance as through a wide breach; amid the ruins they keep pouring in.
Terrors turn against me; my honor is driven away as by the wind, and my prosperity passes like a cloud.
And now my soul melts away within me; days of affliction grip me.
At night pain pierces my bones, and the pains that gnaw me never rest.
With great force He grasps my garment; He seizes me by the collar of my tunic.
He throws me into the mud, and I have become like dust and ashes.
I cry out to You, but You do not answer; I stand up, but You merely stare at me.
You have turned cruel to me; You oppose me with Your strong hand.
You lift me up to the wind and make me ride upon it, and You dissolve me in the storm.
Indeed, I know that You will bring me to death, to the place appointed for all the living.
Yet no one stretches out his hand against a ruined man when he cries for help in his distress.
Did I not weep for those in trouble? Did my soul not grieve for the needy?
But when I hoped for good, evil came; when I waited for light, darkness fell.
My insides churn and will not be still; days of affliction confront me.
I go about darkened, but not by the sun; I stand up in the assembly and cry for help.
I have become a brother of jackals and a companion of ostriches.
My skin grows black and peels off, and my bones burn with fever.
My harp has turned to mourning, and my flute to the sound of those who weep.
